text: Come live in my heart and pay no rent.
author: Samuel Lover

text: She walks in beauty, like the night of cloudless climes and starry skies.
author: Lord Byron

text: I love thee to the depth and breadth and height my soul can reach.
author: Elizabeth Barrett Browning

text: How do I love thee? Let me count the ways.
author: Elizabeth Barrett Browning

text: Thou art my life, my love, my heart, the very eyes of me.
author: Robert Herrick

text: Two souls with but a single thought, two hearts that beat as one.
author: Friedrich Halm

text: Love is not love which alters when it alteration finds.
author: William Shakespeare

text: Shall I compare thee to a summer's day? Thou art more lovely and more temperate.
author: William Shakespeare

text: I am yours, you are mine, of this we are certain. You are lodged in my heart, the small key is lost — you must stay there forever.
author: Goethe

text: My heart is, and always will be, yours.
author: Jane Austen

text: If music be the food of love, play on.
author: William Shakespeare

text: For it was not into my ear you whispered, but into my heart. It was not my lips you kissed, but my soul.
author: Judy Garland

text: I would die for you. But I won't live for you — I'll live beside you, always.
author: Unknown

text: You are the poem I never knew how to write and this life is the story I have always wanted to tell.
author: Tyler Knott Gregson

text: Love is the voice under all silences, the hope which has no opposite in fear.
author: E.E. Cummings

text: The spaces between your fingers were created so that another's could fill them in.
author: Unknown

text: I want to be your favourite hello and your hardest goodbye.
author: Unknown

text: You are the finest, loveliest, tenderest, and most beautiful person I have ever known — and even that is an understatement.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

text: To the world you may be one person, but to one person you may be the world.
author: Bill Wilson

text: At the touch of love, everyone becomes a poet.
author: Plato

text: The steam from your tea cup rises like a secret we share only in these small hours.
author: Unknown

text: Rain on the window, your head on my shoulder, and the world outside feels like a story we don't need to finish.
author: Unknown

text: We pass the salt at dinner the way other people pass secrets.
author: Unknown

text: The quiet click of your keys in the lock tells me the day has ended gently.
author: Unknown

text: Evening settles between us like a shared blanket we never need to adjust.
author: Unknown

text: We sit side by side, each reading our own book, the silence between us full of unspoken understanding.
author: Unknown

text: The way you remember how I take my coffee feels like a secret language only we speak.
author: Unknown

text: Your footsteps in the hallway announce your return, and something in me settles.
author: Unknown

text: In the quiet of shared mornings, your presence turns routine into something tender.
author: Unknown

text: The curve of your back against the dawn light feels like a secret we share with no one.
author: Unknown

text: Nights end softer when your breath syncs with mine in the dark.
author: Unknown

text: In the hush of morning light, your hand finds mine without a word, and the day begins quietly whole.
author: Unknown

text: The way you fold the newspaper after reading leaves space for my own thoughts beside yours.
author: Unknown

text: Even in separate rooms, the knowledge of your nearness turns the air softer around me.
author: Unknown

text: Our hands brush in the dark, finding each other without need for words.
author: Unknown

text: The coffee you make tastes different when you hand it to me without asking.
author: Unknown

text: We share the newspaper, trading sections like quiet agreements on how to begin the day.
author: Unknown
